actions-list.actionItem: Yea and Nay vote pending.
On motion to recommit Failed by the Yeas and Nays: (Roll no. 86).
Considered as unfinished business. H.R. 7744 — "Making appropriations for the Department of Homeland Security for the fiscal year ending September 30, 2026, and for other purposes."
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.
On agreeing to the resolution Failed by the Yeas and Nays: (Roll no. 85).

Considered as unfinished business. H. Con. Res. 38 — "Directing the President pursuant to section 5(c) of the War Powers Resolution to remove United States Armed Forces from unauthorized hostilities in the Islamic Republic of Iran."
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.
On motion to suspend the rules and agree to the resolution Agreed to by the Yeas and Nays (2/3 required): (Roll no. 84).
Considered as unfinished business. H. Res. 1099 — "Reaffirming Iran remains the largest state sponsor of terrorism."

The Chair announced that the unfinished business was the vote on motion to suspend the rules and agree to H. Res. 1099, on agreeing to H. Con. Res. 38, on motion to recommit H.R. 7744, and on passage of the bill.
The House convened, returning from a recess continuing the legislative day of March 5.
The Speaker announced that the House do now recess. The next meeting is subject to the call of the Chair.

At the conclusion of debate on H.R. 7744, the Chair put the question on the motion to recommit and by voice vote, announced the noes had prevailed. Ms. DeLauro demanded the yeas and nays and the Chair postponed further proceedings until a time to be announced.
The previous question on the motion to recommit was ordered pursuant to clause 2(b) of rule XIX.
Ms. DeLauro moved to recommit to the Committee on Appropriations.
The previous question was ordered pursuant to the rule.
The House proceeded with one hour of debate on H.R. 7744.
Rule provides for consideration of H.R. 7744 with 1 hour of general debate. Motion to recommit allowed. Bill is closed to amendments.

Considered under the provisions of rule H. Res. 1095. H.R. 7744 — "Making appropriations for the Department of Homeland Security for the fiscal year ending September 30, 2026, and for other purposes."
The House proceeded with one minute speeches which by direction of the Chair, would be limited to 15 per side of the aisle.
The Chair designated Mr. Correa to lead the Members in reciting the Pledge of Allegiance to the Flag.
The Speaker announced approval of the Journal.  Pursuant to clause 1, rule I, the Journal stands approved.
Today's prayer was offered by the House Chaplain, Chaplain Margaret Grun Kibben.
The House convened, returning from a recess continuing the legislative day of March 5.
The Speaker announced that the House do now recess. The next meeting is scheduled for 12:00 P.M. today.
The House proceeded with Morning-Hour Debate. At the conclusion of Morning-Hour, the House will recess until 12:00 p.m. for the start of legislative business.
The Speaker designated the Honorable Carol D. Miller to act as Speaker pro tempore for today.
The House convened, starting a new legislative day.
